sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: WPP acquires German healthcare specialist 3K Communication
feed.summary: WPP has announced the acquisition of 3K Agentur für Kommunikation (3K), a leading German healthcare specialist PR agency. 3K will join WPP’s Hill+Knowlton Strategies, enhancing H+K’s healthcare presence in Europe and expanding its German operations. This acquisition aligns with WPP's strategic focus on healthcare and is part of its ongoing growth strategy through focused mergers and acquisitions.

feed.title: Sony buying Take-Two Interactive rumors now seem even more dubious
feed.summary: Rumors circulating about Sony's potential acquisition of Take-Two Interactive, parent company of GTA, are increasingly doubtful. This skepticism stems from the questionable track record of the source, a Twitter user named Rythian, who also made unsubstantiated claims about Sony acquiring numerous other major gaming companies. Additionally, Take-Two's recent $12.7 billion acquisition of Zynga significantly increased its valuation, making a buyout by Sony less appealing and more challenging to gain approval.

feed.title: Auto Aftermarket: Magna, LKQ see Q4 growth despite headwinds
feed.summary: Magna International and LKQ Corporation both reported increased revenues in the fourth quarter of 2025, despite challenges like softer vehicle production and broader industry headwinds. Magna saw sales grow to US$10.8 billion, while LKQ's revenue increased to US$3.3 billion, though adjusted earnings for both companies faced pressure. Both companies expressed optimism for 2026, with Magna projecting sales between US$41.9 billion and US$43.5 billion, and LKQ providing adjusted earnings per share guidance of US$2.90 to US$3.20.

feed.title: Manhattan Associates (MANH) Stock Trades Up, Here Is Why
feed.summary: Shares of Manhattan Associates (MANH) jumped 3.2% after the company announced an increase in its share repurchase authority from $100 million to $500 million. This move suggests confidence from leadership in the company's future performance and can increase the value of remaining shares. While the stock has been volatile and is down 8.9% year-to-date, this news indicates a meaningful development for the market's perception of the business.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips (COP) gets PT hike from UBS as oil price forecasts rise
feed.summary: UBS has increased its price target for ConocoPhillips (COP) due to rising oil price forecasts. This indicates a positive outlook from the investment bank for the company's future performance.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Bryce Johns Sells 1,500 Shares of Chubb Ltd (CB)
feed.summary: Bryce Johns, Senior Vice President at Chubb Life, sold 1,500 shares of Chubb Ltd (CB) on March 4, 2026, reducing his holdings to 24,770 shares. The company, a global insurance provider, has seen more insider selling than buying over the past year. Chubb Ltd is currently trading at $336.62, and its price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.18 suggests it is modestly overvalued according to GuruFocus's intrinsic value estimate.

feed.title: Why Citigroup (C) Stock Is Trading Lower Today
feed.summary: Shares of Citigroup (C) fell 2.5% today due to broader market jitters surrounding the health of the private credit market, following events like BlackRock capping withdrawals and liquidity failures at Blue Owl Capital. This caused a sell-off in the financial sector, with the Banking & Investment Services sector declining 2.73%. Citigroup's stock had also dropped 5.8% previously due to hotter-than-expected inflation data and rising credit risk concerns.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips (COP) Gets PT Hike from UBS as Oil Price Forecasts Rise
feed.summary: UBS has increased its price target for ConocoPhillips (NYSE: COP) from $130 to $144, reiterating a Buy rating. The adjustment reflects UBS's revised oil price outlook for 2026, with WTI now expected at $68 and Brent at $72, alongside a modest expansion in valuation multiples due to geopolitical risks, particularly a potential prolonged Middle East conflict. The firm believes companies producing both oil and gas could see significant free cash flow upside if these risks materialize, driving oil and natural gas prices higher.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Linda Dempsey Sells 2,000 Shares of CF Industries Holdings Inc (CF)
feed.summary: Linda Dempsey, Vice President of Public Affairs at CF Industries Holdings Inc (CF), sold 2,000 shares of the company on March 5, 2026. This transaction reduces her total holdings to 17,547 shares. The company's stock was trading at $106.5 per share, making it modestly overvalued according to its GF Value.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Guy Gibson Sells Shares of Northern Trust Corp (NT
feed.summary: Guy Gibson, Co-President Asset Servicing at Northern Trust Corp, sold 3,009 shares of the company on March 5, 2026. This transaction adds to a total of 4,052 shares sold by Gibson over the past year, with no purchases. Northern Trust Corp (NTRS) is currently trading at $142.39, with a price-earnings ratio of 15.86 and a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.29, suggesting it is modestly overvalued.

feed.title: Is MKS (MKSI) Turning Strong China And Singapore Sales Into A Durable Margin Advantage?
feed.summary: MKS Instruments (MKSI) reported a 10.5% year-over-year revenue increase in Q4 2025, driven by strong sales in China and Singapore. Analysts highlight that expanding AI and semiconductor demand, coupled with higher-margin chemistry and services revenue, are boosting earnings despite high leverage and a rich valuation. While recent debt refinancing modestly improves the financial cushion, investors should consider the potential impact of rising trade and tariff risks on international growth.

feed.title: OKX Hits $25B Valuation with NYSE Parent ICE Investment | 2026 - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Cryptocurrency exchange OKX has reached a $25 billion valuation following a minority investment from Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), parent company of the NYSE. This strategic partnership involves ICE licensing spot cryptocurrency pricing data from OKX and introducing U.S.-regulated futures contracts, while OKX will distribute ICE's U.S. futures and tokenized equities markets globally. This move signifies a growing integration of digital assets into mainstream finance and comes amidst broader industry developments and discussions around cryptocurrency legislation.

feed.title: Mineralys Therapeutics Shares Test New Low Amid Sector Pressures ()
feed.summary: Mineralys Therapeutics, a clinical-stage biotechnology firm, has seen its shares drop to a new 52-week low amid broader sector pressures. The company's valuation is heavily dependent on its progress in cardiovascular and metabolic disease drug development and efficient cash management for its ongoing research. Investors are closely watching clinical milestones, study results, and upcoming data releases to assess the company's future potential.

feed.title: Analysts Are Bullish on Top Consumer Goods Stocks: Procter & Gamble (PG), Target (TGT)
feed.summary: Two analysts have issued bullish sentiments on Procter & Gamble (PG) and Target (TGT) within the Consumer Goods sector. RBC Capital maintained a Buy rating on Procter & Gamble with a $172 price target, while RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on Target with a $130 price target. The article details the reasoning behind these ratings and the analysts' track records.

feed.title: J Balvin remade Van Halen's 'Jump' as Coca-Cola's World Cup anthem. He says it was 'a puzzle'
feed.summary: J Balvin has reimagined Van Halen's iconic song "Jump" as Coca-Cola's anthem for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, alongside Travis Barker, Amber Mark, and Steve Vai. Balvin initially felt hesitant about touching such a classic but wanted to infuse it with Latin passion for fútbol by adding a new Spanish verse. This collaboration, released under Coca-Cola's new Real Thing Records label, aims to unite people globally through the spirit of the World Cup, despite Balvin's concerns about the US immigration stance potentially affecting the tournament.

feed.title: O'Reilly Automotive (ORLY) Announces $850 Million Senior Notes Offering
feed.summary: O'Reilly Automotive (ORLY) has announced an $850 million offering of 5.100% Senior Notes due 2036, with an estimated net proceeds of $841 million. The company plans to use these funds to repay existing notes due in 2026, reduce commercial paper liabilities, and for general corporate purposes including potential acquisitions and share repurchases. This financial move aims to optimize O'Reilly Automotive's debt structure and support its future growth initiatives.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Philip Laskawy Sells Shares of Henry Schein Inc (H
feed.summary: Philip Laskawy, a Director at Henry Schein Inc (HSIC), sold 2,844 shares on March 5, 2026, reducing his holding to 21,961 shares. This transaction follows a pattern of insider sells for HSIC, with 5 sells and 1 buy over the past year. Henry Schein Inc, a healthcare products and services provider, is currently trading at $80.36, which is considered fairly valued according to its GF Value.

feed.title: Sundar Pichai Sells 32,500 Shares of Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) Stock
feed.summary: Sundar Pichai recently sold 32,500 shares of Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) stock for approximately $9.86 million, reducing his stake by 1.90%. This transaction follows several other sales by Pichai in recent months. Alphabet reported strong Q4 earnings, beating revenue and EPS estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.21.

feed.title: Krystal Biotech (NASDAQ:KRYS) Insider Sells $6,580,250.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Suma Krishnan, an insider at Krystal Biotech (NASDAQ:KRYS), sold 25,000 shares of the company's stock for $6,580,250 on March 4th, reducing her ownership by 1.75%. This transaction comes after Krystal Biotech reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations, and maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $326.25. The stock experienced a slight dip of 2.6% to $253.69 following the sale.

feed.title: Insider Buying: LTC Properties (NYSE:LTC) Director Buys 7,000 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: LTC Properties (NYSE:LTC) Director David Gruber recently purchased 7,000 shares of the company's stock for $266,000, increasing his total holdings by over 23%. This insider buying follows a strong earnings report where LTC Properties exceeded EPS consensus and saw a significant year-over-year revenue increase. The company also announced a monthly dividend, offering a 5.9% yield, and received a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts.

feed.title: David Carter Sells 4,000 Shares of Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (NASDAQ:KTOS) Stock
feed.summary: David Carter, an insider at Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (NASDAQ:KTOS), sold 4,000 shares of the company's stock for $341,440 on March 5th, reducing his stake by 4.65%. This sale, along with others from CFO Deanna Lund, is viewed by some investors as a near-term negative signal despite Kratos beating quarterly earnings expectations and securing new contracts. The company faces high valuation and volatility risks, even with strong analyst support and recent positive developments like the acquisition of Orbit Technologies.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Henry Schein (NASDAQ:HSIC) EVP Sells $478,740.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Henry Schein (NASDAQ:HSIC) EVP Mark Mlotek sold 5,925 shares of the company's stock for $478,740.00 on March 4, reducing his stake by 6.85%. This sale occurred as the stock traded down 3.2% to $77.60, despite the company beating Q4 earnings estimates with $1.34 EPS and $3.44 billion in revenue and setting positive FY2026 guidance. Analyst ratings for HSIC are mixed, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average price target of $85.58.

feed.title: Quanta Services Inc.: The Quiet Stock Powering America’s AI Grid
feed.summary: Quanta Services Inc. is presented as a crucial, understated player in the infrastructure essential for AI, clean energy, and EV expansion in the US. The article highlights Quanta's role in building power lines, substations, and fiber networks for utilities and data centers. It positions the company as a "picks and shovels" investment, benefiting from long-term secular trends and substantial government and private spending on grid modernization and electrification.

feed.title: NASDAQ: PYPL: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP Announces the Filing of a Securities Fraud Class Action Lawsuit Against PayPal Holdings, Inc.
feed.summary: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP has filed a securities fraud class action lawsuit against PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL). The lawsuit targets investors who purchased PayPal common stock between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026, alleging that the company made material misstatements and omissions regarding its projected revenue outlook and growth. Investors have until April 20, 2026, to seek lead plaintiff status in the case.

feed.title: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:MDGL) General Counsel Sells $155,498.40 in Stock
feed.summary: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals' General Counsel, Shannon Kelley, sold 360 shares of company stock for $155,498.40 on March 6, 2026, reducing her holdings by 2.88%. The transaction was disclosed in an SEC filing. Despite beating revenue estimates, the company missed EPS expectations and remains unprofitable with negative ROE and net margin, with shares trading below their 50- and 200-day moving averages.

feed.title: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:MDGL) CFO Sells $853,984.34 in Stock
feed.summary: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals' CFO, Mardi Dier, sold 1,982 shares of company stock for over $853,000 on March 6th, reducing her stake by 13.7%. Despite the company missing GAAP EPS estimates in its latest earnings report, revenue exceeded expectations, and analysts remain bullish with an average price target of $685.31. The stock traded down about 1.5% following the earnings miss.

feed.title: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:MDGL) CEO Sells $718,316.22 in Stock
feed.summary: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals CEO William John Sibold sold 1,663 shares of the company's stock for $718,316.22, reducing his stake by 1.02%. This transaction follows a previous sale in January. Despite missing EPS estimates, the company reported increased revenue, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $685.31.

feed.title: Methode Electronics Balances Growth Ambitions With Margin Strain
feed.summary: Methode Electronics' recent earnings call revealed a combination of strong cash generation and growth in industrial and data center markets, alongside significant margin pressures. The company is actively rationalizing its portfolio and reducing capital expenditure to protect cash flow, but is facing challenges from North American automotive weakness and operational issues in Mexico, leading to a cut in adjusted EBITDA guidance.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Matson (NYSE:MATX) EVP Sells 8,603 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Matson (NYSE:MATX) EVP John Lauer sold 8,603 shares of the company's stock on March 5th, 2026, for a total of approximately $1.43 million, reducing his holding by 26.6%. This insider selling occurred despite Matson reporting better-than-expected earnings of $4.60 EPS against a $3.69 consensus, though revenue declined 4.3% year-over-year. Following the news, shares traded down about 3.7% to $152.94, with institutional investors holding 84.76% of the company's stock.

feed.title: Institutional Shifts Challenge Consensus On Equity Residential’s Earnings Outlook
feed.summary: Institutional investors are showing mixed signals regarding Equity Residential (NYSE:EQR), with some like APG Asset Management and Erste Asset Management reducing stakes, while Dimensional Fund Advisors increased its position. This divergence, coupled with varied analyst opinions, suggests a lack of clear consensus on the company's future outlook. Investors are advised to consider their own thesis on EQR, focusing on factors like rental markets, balance sheet strength, and income stability, particularly in light of upcoming management commentary at Citi’s Global Property CEO Conference.

feed.title: State regulators give tentative approval to Aquarion Water Co. sale amid concerns over rates, oversight
feed.summary: Connecticut's Public Utilities Regulatory Authority (PURA) has given preliminary approval to the acquisition of Aquarion Water Co. by the South Central Connecticut Regional Water Authority, despite concerns from opponents regarding governance, rate-setting, and rate impact. Attorney General William Tong strongly criticized the decision, calling it an "economic disaster" due to projected transaction costs. However, Aquarion and the Regional Water Authority leadership view the decision as a constructive step, citing potential benefits from lower-cost capital and commitments to stabilize rates and support municipalities.

feed.title: Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) SVP Sells 2,628 Shares
feed.summary: Karthik Kuppusamy, SVP at Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX), sold 2,628 shares of the company's stock on March 4th for over $538,000, reducing his stake by 16.35%. This transaction comes as Quest Diagnostics recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and announced a $1.0 billion share buyback program and an increased quarterly dividend. Analysts currently rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $210.29.

feed.title: Duke Energy launches $6 billion at-the-market equity program
feed.summary: Duke Energy Corporation has initiated an at-the-market equity distribution program to sell up to $6 billion of its common stock through various financial institutions. This move comes as the electric utility manages a significant debt burden and its stock trades near a 52-week high, despite InvestingPro analysis suggesting it is overvalued. The program aims to raise capital, with proceeds used by the company after commissions and expenses.

feed.title: Ex-KEPCO Worker Steals 12km Cables Amid Copper Surge
feed.summary: A former KEPCO employee has been accused of stealing 12 kilometers of copper cables. This theft occurred amid a surge in copper prices, indicating a potential motive for the crime.

feed.title: Incyte Corporation (INCY) Receives FDA Complete Response Letter for Zynyz® sBLA
feed.summary: Incyte Corporation (INCY) received a Complete Response Letter (CRL) from the FDA for its Zynyz® sBLA for metastatic non-small cell lung cancer, citing regulatory compliance issues at a third-party manufacturing facility. The FDA's decision was not based on concerns about the drug's efficacy or safety. Incyte is working with the FDA and the facility to address the issues for a potential resubmission.

feed.title: The Woman Behind a Trouble Call
feed.summary: Alissa Osburn, a troubleman field supervisor at Southern California Edison, shares her 17-year journey in the electric industry, moving from meter reader to substation operator and system operator before her current field role. Despite the male-dominated environment, Osburn advocates for curiosity and determination, encouraging women to explore challenging careers. Her experiences reinforce the value of continuous learning and building trust in specialized fields.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Ralph Larossa Sells Shares of Public Service Enter
feed.summary: Ralph Larossa, Chair, President, and CEO of Public Service Electric and Gas Company, a subsidiary of Public Service Enterprise Group Inc (PEG), sold 2,083 shares of the company on March 5, 2026. This transaction increased his total holdings to 292,889.0003 shares. Over the past year, Larossa has sold 7,604 shares with no purchases, and the company has seen seven insider sells and no buys.

feed.title: Skyworks Solutions: The Quiet Chip Powerhouse Wall Street Is Watching
feed.summary: Skyworks Solutions, an Apple supplier and semiconductor company, is being watched by Wall Street as a potential comeback story due to its integral role in 5G, Wi-Fi 7, and connected devices despite being a "quiet" player. The article discusses its exposure to Apple, dividend, cash flow, and valuation compared to AI giants, while also acknowledging risks like dependence on Apple and competition. For investors, Skyworks represents a way to play connectivity growth without the volatility of highly hyped tech stocks.

feed.title: Lamb Weston (LW) to Join S&P 600 in Upcoming Index Rebalance
feed.summary: Lamb Weston (LW), a leading producer of frozen potato products, is set to join the S&P 600 index before market open on Monday, March 23, 2026, as part of a routine quarterly rebalancing. This inclusion acknowledges the company's strong market performance and potentially influences investor perception. While Lamb Weston demonstrates impressive financial performance with significant revenue growth and profitability, concerns exist regarding its debt levels and asset efficiency.

feed.title: Updating Moat Ratings and Fair Value Estimates for Payroll Firms
feed.summary: Morningstar has updated its economic moat ratings and fair value estimates for several payroll firms due to the potential impact of large language models and agentic AI. The firm believes that these technological advancements introduce heightened uncertainty to the return structure of these businesses over the next 10-20 years, driven by more application layer economics, less evidence of current AI monetization, and exposure to seat-based pricing. As a result, Morningstar is downgrading the moat ratings for ADP and Paychex to narrow from wide, and for Paycom to none from narrow, while also lowering the fair value estimates for ADP and Paychex.

feed.title: Institutional Shifts Challenge Consensus On Equity Residential’s Earnings Outlook
feed.summary: Institutional investors are showing mixed signals regarding Equity Residential (NYSE:EQR), with some like APG Asset Management US Inc. and Erste Asset Management GmbH reducing stakes, while Dimensional Fund Advisors LP has increased its position. This, coupled with split analyst activity, suggests a lack of clear consensus on the company's future earnings outlook. Shareholders are advised to scrutinize their own investment thesis, considering factors like rental markets, balance sheet strength, and income stability as the company prepares to address these cross-currents at an upcoming conference.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser to Release Third Quarter Results on October 24
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser Company (NYSE: WY) announced it will release its third-quarter 2024 results on Thursday, October 24, after the market closes. The company will host a live webcast and conference call on Friday, October 25, at 7 a.m. Pacific (10 a.m. Eastern) to discuss the results. Weyerhaeuser is a major owner of timberlands and a manufacturer of wood products, operating as a real estate investment trust.

feed.title: Capital One to eliminate 1,139 jobs connected with former Discover facility in Riverwoods
feed.summary: Capital One plans to eliminate 1,139 jobs associated with the former Discover Financial facility in Riverwoods, Illinois, following its merger with Discover. These job cuts include employees working at the site, as well as remote Illinois and out-of-state residents reporting to Riverwoods-based teams. The company stated that customer-facing roles are not affected and is providing support to impacted employees, with separations expected to begin in May 2026.

feed.title: Zimcal Asset Management recently pointed out that the current market value of Medallion Financial Corp. (Medallion) does not reflect its true worth, and directly stated that the company's development is hindered by inefficient management from its leadership.
feed.summary: Zimcal Asset Management believes that Medallion Financial Corp.'s current market value does not reflect its true worth due to inefficient management. The asset management firm suggests that replacing the core management team could fully unleash the financial enterprise's potential. This statement indicates a call for significant leadership changes to improve the company's performance and valuation.

feed.title: Mdxhealth Earnings Call Highlights Fast Growth, Rising Costs
feed.summary: Mdxhealth's recent Q4 earnings call revealed significant growth, with total billable test volumes up 62% year over year, driven largely by liquid-based assays. Despite this revenue growth to $29.5 million, the company experienced deepening operating and net losses due to acquisition-related costs and integration expenses from the ExoDx deal. Management remains confident in achieving meaningful profitability by 2026, targeting $137–$140 million in revenue and a 10% adjusted EBITDA margin by the end of that year.

feed.title: Montrose Environmental Posts Record Results, Lifts 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: Montrose Environmental Group (MEG) reported record revenue and EBITDA for 2025, exceeding initial outlooks and demonstrating strong organic growth and cash generation. The company lifted its 2026 outlook, projecting higher EBITDA and margins, driven by secular demand in environmental services and improved cross-selling. Despite some near-term margin softness and seasonal volatility in Q4, Montrose is focused on long-term growth, strategic investments, and a more shareholder-friendly capital allocation approach.

feed.title: Ellis, general counsel, sells Kinetik Holdings (KNTK) shares for $59,119
feed.summary: Lindsay Ellis, General Counsel at Kinetik Holdings Inc (NASDAQ:KNTK), sold 1,260 shares for $59,119 on March 4, 2026. This sale was conducted to cover tax obligations from vested shares awarded for the 2025 annual incentive. Kinetik Holdings, which offers a 7.1% dividend yield, recently reported a significant EPS beat but a revenue miss for Q4 2025.

feed.title: Howard, Kinetik Holdings CFO, sells $75963 in shares
feed.summary: Kinetik Holdings Inc.'s CFO, Trevor Howard, sold 1,619 shares of Class A Common Stock for $75,963 to cover tax withholding obligations. This transaction occurred after the company reported strong Q4 2025 EPS despite a revenue shortfall. Howard directly owns 249,795 shares following the sale.

feed.title: Western Digital Corp. Hits Day Low at $244.42 Amid Price Pressure
feed.summary: Western Digital Corp. experienced a significant stock decline, dropping 7.21% to an intraday low of $244.42 on March 3, 2026. Despite recent negative performance over the past week and month, the company maintains strong long-term fundamentals, including 55.83% net profit growth in December 2025, five consecutive quarters of positive results, and robust operating cash flow and return on capital employed. Its low debt-to-equity ratio and high return on equity also highlight its financial resilience.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE:PEG) CEO Sells 2,083 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE:PEG) CEO Ralph Larossa sold 2,083 shares of the company's stock for approximately $174,264, reducing his total stake by 0.71%. This transaction follows the company's recent strong performance, including a slight quarterly EPS beat, 18.3% revenue increase, raised FY2026 guidance, and an increased quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus price target of $92.73.
